title: CAHOOTS - 211info
text: WHITE BIRD CLINIC CAHOOTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mobile crisis intervention services. Responds to a range of non-criminal crises, including intoxication, disorientation, substance use and mental health issues, dispute resolution, and street facilitation. Transport to treatment is also provided. Service is confidential, voluntary, and staffed and managed by White Bird Clinic. Home visits available. 323 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: People who require crisis intervention Hours: 7 days per week 11am-11pm; service hours may vary depending on staff availability Email (541) 726-3714 - Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (541) 726-3714 Non-emergency Dispatch Springfield Police Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Services are accessed by calling non-emergency police dispatch. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: City of Springfield SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Downtown on 12th Avenue between High Street and Mill Street Services: In Person Crisis Intervention Public Inebriate Transportation Substance Use Disorder Crisis Intervention Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - BIRTH CERTIFICATES HOUSING/SHELTER - LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - NEST MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - CRISIS COUNSELING HOTLINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. WHITE BIRD CLINIC
